tokens: Rover readouts, Joe: 72 (on heading) 0 on bearing; distance (driven), 0.2; range (to the Nav initialization point), 0:0; amp-hours, 90, 95, 100, 105; and motor temps are still off-scale, low.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Head about, oh, I'd say 270, until they give us an update.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Head a little to the north...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We're going around the ALSEP, too.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Yeah, let's not drive through the ALSEP.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It's too bad the (16-mm) camera didn't work because there was some neat bumps there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Look...Is that a glass ball right there?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Right on top of the surface...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Almost like a black spherule of glass.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Right now we're swinging around more to the west.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...passing the Quark Triplet that's on your right, probably, and we'll be most likely be directing you back towards the western crater in that triplet for some mare sampling towards the end.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I see them and they look rather fresh.
There's a lot of angular light-colored fragments on the rim, Joe.
So, mark our position here; we're (at) bearing 110 and range, 0.2.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We dropped into a shallow depression there, and that was the Quark Triplet there on the northwest side of that shallow depression.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Oop, look at this nice, little, new fresh one.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: But there're not too many fragments on the rim.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: There's a very large depression ahead of us.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We don't want to drive through that.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I don't know whether we do or not.
Let's take a look at it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Oh, I wouldn't think so, Dave.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Let's take a look at it.
Look at the big boulder there, Jim.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'd say, let's go north of it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I really can't tell how wide it is, but at the very deepest portion of it, it looks like there's a crater.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I get the idea that it looks collapsed north to south, doesn't it?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I don't (know)...Look at our map here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Let's see, we're going about 8 clicks.
(Pause) And we're kind of dropping down as we go around the (depression).
And we're heading 320; we're on the northeast rim of this very shallow depression.
By "shallow", (I mean that) the slopes are probably 3 degrees.
And I guess, at the deepest part there, it's probably, oh, 200 feet (60 meters) deep.
(Pause) Right now, we're on the north side of that depression.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (To Houston) And we're swinging around to the west, heading, 270.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Good name for a big crater.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, bearing is 113, and we're at 0.6.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: There's another big one (meaning another large depression), Jim.
Whoo, and look at that rock over there.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...a block angular frag on the northwest side about one-quarter of the way down...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We've got to drive right by it, anyway.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, Jim, don't hesitate to fire off pictures right and left here.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Oh, I wish I could, Joe!
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I bet it chipped that hole, Jim.
It went right in...It came from that...It made that crater there.
And it come from 250, (correcting himself) I mean (laughing) 070.
That angular projectile about a foot across, Joe, had made a secondary about a meter across, and it came from a 070 heading.
I bet you anything, because the...Oh, that was neat!
One part of the frag was covered with glass, and the central part of the crater was covered with glass.
Obviously a secondary, and obviously made by that angular frag.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, we've got another shallow depression here up ahead.
And I don't know whether...I'd say, we'd be better off staying to the north, wouldn't you?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: A fresh one (meaning a fresh crater) out at 1 o'clock.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It looks like a large fresh one.
There are a lot of angular, light-colored blocks on its rim.
You going to try going through here, huh?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Down here to the left, it looks pretty flat.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Okay, we're heading through another shallow depression, similar to the last large depression that we described.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: What's that fragment at 12 o'clock to us?
Another piece of glass, I suppose.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Pause) Another glassy fragment, angular, about 3 inches long, sitting right on the surface.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, you know, the surface is smooth, but it's pretty rough out here.
Smooth on a small scale, and there's lots of...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: You really could get lost here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Going up to the Front, you could always look back and see the LM.
Here, it's like driving over the big sand dunes in the desert.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Pretty rough on the one you're seeing now.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And you're looking for Nav readings of 1.8 clicks at 088, when you're at Station 9.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: And now there's very large shallow depression.
And, Dave, they're all about the same size!
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Go around the south of this one.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think they're, you know, the first really shallow depressions or very subdued craters we've seen.
And there are just three in a line that run east-west.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, I'd say they're probably about 100 meters across and maybe, what, 10, 15 meters deep?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, I thought we'd whip right over to the rille.
I didn't think we'd have this type of terrain!
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Oh, it was a lot easier driving yesterday.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Look at that rock over at, oh, 1 o'clock.
Well, it was on the horizon; like kind of a pedestal.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: You can see a lot of them, right on the horizon.
We probably want to, when you can, Dave, swing around to the west; we're going a little too far south here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Say again the coordinates, Joe, of the station.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Dave, you're driving towards 1.8 clicks; 088, bearing.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: We're (at a bearing of) 092, now; heading slightly south.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Well, what I'm thinking, there's nothing unique about (the exact location of) Station 9, is there?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Scarp Crater is over this way.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I was thinking, you know, just a good sample point...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: And, we can minimize the distance.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (Joking) Just don't drive too far west (and fall into the rille).
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I was going to say, I thought I could see it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, Joe, we'll let you know when the slope gets up to 26.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I think I can see the far side of the rille, now.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I think we're coming up on the rim of it.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Take a little jog over here to where it's a little smoother.
(Long Pause) It's a steep slope, isn't it?
Yep, I think we (garbled) No, it's another fresh crater.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And, you do kind of get the impression there's a rim here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Off to the left there, the higher part.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: There's rough terrain ahead of us.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We dropped down into another little valley.
There's another one of those shallow depressions off at 1 o'clock.
Right now our bearing is 89, range 1.4.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: This must be...I'll go around to the left here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Or towards the right, [it's] fairly smooth on the right.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: (To Houston) Okay, we're heading down into another depression.
It has oh, one, two, three other recent craters.
The one of the southern rim looks to be the most recent.
In fact, it's kind of a doublet with a smaller crater in the north rim of it.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Well, look at the two here.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: Another doublet there on the left.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Right now, we're heading, oh, about 250.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Boy, look at the fresh blocks ahead of us.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: ...You must be very near Scarp Crater.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: I was going to say, that's probably Scarp Crater.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: It sure kicked up a lot of rocks.
What are you going to do, go on the north side of it?
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: I want to take a look and see if that's it.
Boy, it's really fresh with a lot of debris.
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I'd say this is probably Scarp Crater, wouldn't you?
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: I would, because we can definitely see the far side of the...
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: We could drive back to this one.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Yeah, but this is so much ...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: ...and with any luck at all, that is Scarp Crater.
transcript.speaker: Allen

tokens: Yeah, let's do this one anyway, Jim.
Wait a minute, let me get to where I can...
transcript.speaker: Scott

tokens: And we can definitely see the west side of the rille from here.
transcript.speaker: Irwin

tokens: Probably see, oh, 10 to 15 percent of the (rille wall on the) far side.
transcript.speaker: Irwin
